senses.definition: physical exercise done in preparation for a sports competition

examples.x: He needs more training to improve his swimming speed.
I missed my tennis training because of the rain.
The coach insisted on extra training for the basketball team.
The soccer team has daily training before the big match.
Yoga is a good training for flexibility and balance.

senses.definition: the act of showing respect, fairness, and kindness to others while participating in sports or games, regardless of the outcome

examples.x: Even though their team lost, Mike showed great sportsmanship by congratulating the winners.
It is important to teach children sportsmanship so they understand the value of playing fairly.
At the tournament, the players demonstrated true sportsmanship by helping each other after the game.
She lost the race but displayed excellent sportsmanship by shaking hands with her opponents.
The coach emphasized sportsmanship during the match, reminding everyone to respect the referee's decisions.

senses.definition: a short break between two halves of a game or match

examples.x: Half-time lasted fifteen minutes before the game resumed.

senses.definition: an act in a sport that is against the rules and is not allowed

examples.x: The player was penalized for a foul after tripping his opponent.
The referee called a foul for pushing during the match.
A yellow card was issued for a dangerous foul in soccer.
The athlete's foul led to a disqualification in the race.

senses.definition: a deceptive move made by a player to mislead opponents about their intended action or direction

examples.x: He used a head fake to throw off the defender and score.
The tennis player's head fake drew her opponent out of position.
The quarterback's head fake fooled the entire defense.
Using a head fake, the boxer set up a powerful punch.
With a quick head fake, he evaded the oncoming tackler.

senses.definition: a league of the highest-ranking in a particular sport, especially baseball

examples.x: He dreams of playing in a major league one day and competing with the best athletes in the world.
The major league baseball season starts in spring, drawing crowds from all over the country.
The team made it to the major league after years of hard work and dedication.
After watching the major league game, he decided to pursue a career in professional sports.
Many young players aspire to join a major league team, but it takes a lot of practice and perseverance.

senses.definition: a level of professional sports competition below the highest level, typically where younger or less experienced players develop their skills

examples.x: He played in the minor leagues for three seasons before getting called up to the majors.
The minor league team struggled to fill seats despite their strong performance.
Many promising athletes start their careers in the minor leagues to gain experience.
The minor league championship game drew scouts from several major league teams.
She hoped to make a name for herself in the minor leagues before advancing to the majors.

senses.definition: a designated area where a sport or game is played

examples.x: The playing field is divided into two halves during a match.
The children enjoyed playing soccer on the playing field after school.
The playing field was well-maintained for the championship game.
She watched the game from the sidelines near the playing field.
The playing field was muddy after the rain.

senses.definition: (sports) a sequence of games or matches played between the same teams or individuals

examples.x: The basketball series went to a seventh game to determine the champion.
Record-breaking performances highlighted the swimming series.
Dominance in every match marked the home team's volleyball series.
Global participation highlighted the motorsport series this year.
Spanning continents, the golf series featured prestigious international tournaments.

senses.definition: the period before the start of the official sports season when teams practice and play exhibition games

examples.x: The team worked hard during the preseason to improve their skills.
Injuries in preseason can affect a player's performance all year.
The coach implemented new strategies during the preseason.

senses.definition: the selection of a player by a team during a draft process

examples.x: The team made a strategic draft pick in the first round.
The draft pick brought fresh talent to the roster.
He was a highly anticipated draft pick for the upcoming season.
With the first overall draft pick, they chose a promising young athlete.
The draft pick turned out to be a star player.

senses.definition: the additional minutes added to the end of a match to compensate for time lost due to injuries or stoppages

examples.x: The referee added three minutes of injury time to the match.
The team scored a last-minute goal during injury time.
They managed to hold onto their lead until the end of injury time.
The crowd anxiously watched as injury time ticked away.
A player went down with a cramp, leading to more injury time.

senses.definition: a piece of land used for playing a game or sport on

examples.x: After the game, the players left their equipment on the field.
Our picnic was ruined when a baseball game started on the field.
The field for football in our town is well maintained.
The runner collapsed on the track field due to exhaustion.
The soccer team practices on the field behind the school.

senses.definition: to remove a player from active play, typically having them sit on the sidelines

examples.x: The coach benched the star player for disciplinary reasons.
If you keep disobeying team rules, you'll be benched.
He has been benched for the last few games due to poor form.
They had benched him in previous seasons, but he's proven himself now.
She benched herself voluntarily to give her teammates a chance to play.

senses.definition: to attack or advance forcefully towards the opponent

examples.x: The forward decided to aggress the defense early in the game.
They planned to aggress the rival team with high pressure.
To win, we must continuously aggress their defensive line.

senses.definition: to do physical activities or sports to stay healthy and become stronger

examples.x: He doesn't exercise as much as he should.
He needs to exercise more to lose weight.
I try to exercise regularly to keep fit.
We usually exercise in the morning to start our day energetically.

senses.definition: to put a player in a specific position or role during a game

examples.x: The coach decided to field the rookie striker in today's match.
They fielded their strongest lineup for the championship game.
She was fielded as a defender despite her usual position as a midfielder.
They fielded an international player for the first time in their history.
The coach fielded a surprise substitution in the final minutes.

senses.definition: (of a team sport) being short of the usual number of players
related to activities or discussions that occur after a sporting event has concluded

examples.x: The team gathered for a post-game celebration after their victory.
Post-game interviews with players provided insight into their strategies.
Fans eagerly awaited the post-game analysis from sports pundits.
After the post-game meal, players dispersed to rest and recover.
Coaches often use the post-game period to review performance and plan for the next match.

senses.definition: away from the roads and tracks and across the countryside
the act of withdrawing oneself from an event or activity before finishing it

examples.x: The swimmer's DNF surprised everyone since she was the favorite to win.
His coach encouraged him to learn from his DNF and come back stronger in the next competition.
The triathlete's DNF was a reminder of the unpredictable nature of endurance sports.
She felt disappointed with her DNF but remained determined to improve for future races.
His unexpected DNF disappointed his fans who had gathered to cheer him on.

senses.definition: the skillful movement and positioning of the feet to enhance performance and maintain balance in dancing or in sports, especially in boxing and soccer

examples.x: The boxer's excellent footwork allowed him to dodge punches and counterattack effectively.
Her quick footwork helped her navigate through the defenders and score a goal.
The fencer's footwork was impeccable, allowing her to control the distance and strike at the perfect moment.
In dance, precise footwork is key to executing complex routines gracefully.
Proper footwork and stance are important in golf to ensure a balanced and powerful swing.

senses.definition: a position where a player brings their limbs close to their body, often used in gymnastics or diving to execute specific maneuvers

examples.x: They practiced achieving a tight tuck for better control in the air.
The gymnast maintained a compact tuck during her routine.
She showed excellent form by holding her tuck until entry.
He struggled with maintaining a stable tuck during the flip.
The coach praised her for improving her tuck technique.

senses.definition: the back boundary line of a playing area, especially in sports like tennis, volleyball, or badminton

examples.x: The tennis player hit a powerful shot that landed just inside the baseline.
The volleyball player served from behind the baseline.
She positioned herself close to the baseline to return the serve.
The badminton shuttlecock landed just on the baseline, earning a point.
The soccer goalie stayed near the baseline to defend against long shots.

senses.definition: the boundary line that marks the edges of the playing field or court in sports

examples.x: The coach shouted instructions from the sideline.
The football player stepped out of bounds near the sideline.
She cheered loudly from the sideline during the soccer game.
Fans crowded along the sideline, hoping to get a glimpse of their favorite players.
The basketball player made an incredible save to keep the ball in play near the sideline.

senses.definition: to prevent someone from participating in an activity, especially a sport, usually due to injury or other reasons

examples.x: The coach decided to sideline him after he sprained his ankle.
A severe knee injury sidelined the star player for the entire season.
The team had to sideline their leading scorer due to disciplinary issues.
Persistent back pain sidelined her during the championship game.
His recurring hamstring problem sidelined him for most of the year.

senses.definition: (in sports) to swing and miss while attempting to hit a ball

examples.x: Despite his best efforts, he whiffed at the baseball pitch and struck out.
With a powerful swing, he whiffed at the tennis ball, narrowly missing it.
The batter whiffed at the fastball, unable to make solid contact.
The golfer whiffed at the tee shot, causing the ball to roll off course.

senses.definition: the act of initially delivering the ball to start play, such as in tennis, volleyball, or table tennis

examples.x: Her consistent service in badminton put pressure on the opponents.
His tennis service was powerful and difficult to return.
His service was faulted due to a foot fault.
She won the point with an ace during her table tennis service.
The volleyball team focused on improving their service accuracy.

senses.definition: a dynamic athletic maneuver where the legs cross over each other alternately, commonly used in sports such as soccer, swimming, karate, and gymnastics

examples.x: Synchronized swimmers synchronized their scissor kicks perfectly with the music.
The martial artist's scissor kick was both powerful and precise.
He cleared the ball with a well-timed scissor kick to prevent a goal.
Gymnasts practice the scissor kick as a foundational move on the balance beam.
The acrobat's scissor kick added a breathtaking element to his aerial routine.

senses.definition: the opportunity in which player or a team gets to play offense

examples.x: During the power play, it's crucial for the hockey team to capitalize on their turn.
Each team gets a turn to serve the ball in volleyball.
The opposing team will have their turn to bat next inning.
The boxer waited patiently for his turn to strike a decisive blow.
The rugby team's turn to advance with possession led to a try.

senses.definition: (sports) an attempt to score or achieve a goal by kicking, throwing, hitting, or shooting a ball or object toward a target

examples.x: He took a powerful shot from outside the penalty area and scored a goal.
The golfer's putt was a critical shot to save par on the final hole.
The hockey player's slap shot hit the top corner of the net for a goal.

senses.definition: the immediate playback of an important moment in a sports competition on television, usually shown in slow motion

examples.x: The referee used an instant replay to check if the goal was offside.
After the incredible dunk, the announcer called for an instant replay to show the crowd.
During the basketball game, the instant replay helped confirm the foul was committed.
The soccer match paused for an instant replay of the controversial penalty kick.
The football team’s coach asked for an instant replay to review the touchdown.

senses.definition: a system used in sports like football to determine if the ball has crossed the goal line, ensuring accurate goal decisions

examples.x: Goal-line technology helps referees decide if a goal has been scored.
The game relies on goal-line technology for fair play.
Referees trust goal-line technology for accurate calls.
Cameras in the goalposts assist with goal-line technology.
Spectators appreciate the clarity brought by goal-line technology.

senses.definition: to initiate play in a sport by hitting a ball or object from a designated location using a specific technique, as dictated by the rules of the game

examples.x: He stepped up to serve, aiming for the corner of the opponent's court.
Who's serving?
He practiced for hours to improve his ability to serve in tennis.
The match resumed as the player served the shuttlecock into the air.
The player served the volleyball over the net, setting the pace for the game.
She served the tennis ball with a powerful spin to start the match.

senses.definition: a division of time in a game or sport, usually consisting of four equal parts

examples.x: The team scored two touchdowns in the final quarter of the game.
The basketball game was tied at the end of the third quarter.
The fourth quarter was intense, with both teams fighting for the lead.
